content:
Character: Alaric Voss
Perception: Alaric is tall and angular, with silvering temples and the careful dress of someone who once had money or at least taste — scarves, linen, interesting shoes. He has an intellectual bearing and a melancholy I recognize. He came into Sato once, sat at the counter, and watched me work for the entire meal without speaking until the end, when he said my knife work reminded him of Glenn Gould's hands on the keyboard. It was the most perceptive compliment I have received in New York. We have since shared perhaps five quiet conversations on the rooftop. He is a composer, I think, or was. I see him as a fellow devotee of craft who understands what obsession costs. I know he lives on the sixth floor and sometimes plays music late at night that I can faintly hear through the building's bones.
summary:

It was the most perceptive compliment I have received in New York. We have since shared perhaps five quiet conversations on the rooftop. He is a composer, I think, or was.
